Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a young, curious Italian man who spends some time on social media but not excessively, I've been thinking about how we deal with fake content online. It's frustrating when you see misleading information that can spread quickly, especially since my city, Bologna, is part of Italy which has a rich history and sometimes gets caught up in viral hoaxes. With moderate conscientiousness and high extraversion from the Big Five traits, I enjoy exploring topics but also need reliable facts to base my opinions on.

My parents' educational background—my father with upper secondary and my mother with a PhD—has influenced how I approach information. When I was younger, hearing about fake news made me skeptical because of my high openness score, which helps in questioning things that don't add up. Now as a full-time student, I've learned to fact-check more often.

The best way is through critical thinking and cross-referencing sources with reputable organizations or people who have expertise.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
Step-by-step: First, identify the source of information; second, check for multiple reliable perspectives; third, use tools like Snopes or official websites to verify claims; fourth, report misinformation when possible. This method ensures that fake content doesn't influence personal understanding unnecessarily.

Critical thinking helps filter out obvious falsehoods quickly due to high openness and curiosity traits.
